headline: FIRST AGENT BASELINE for Monaco (rotation backfill, never assessed). Published composite 51.6 / Functional (rank 45) sustained against compound evidence: (1) constitutional press freedom respected by government in operational practice, with statutory exception for lèse-majesté (insults/offences against Prince and family — up to 5 years' imprisonment); (2) GRECO oversight of Council of Europe Second Compliance Group ongoing; March 2026 GRECO citation of improvements in legislative-process transparency but persistent gap on parliamentarian financial-interest disclosure and judicial-appointment transparency; (3) July 2024 adoption of asset-and-interest-disclosure regime, gift-reporting rule, and independent ethics committee for executive — positive accountability movement; (4) UN Committee on Elimination of Racial Discrimination (CERD) accepting follow-up submissions for Monaco's 118th-session review (in-window concern indicator) — Monaco under active international human-rights scrutiny; (5) Migrant-worker context: majority of Monaco residents are non-citizens (foreign-national majority population structure); union and collective-bargaining rights protected for foreign workers; government workers prohibited from striking; (6) no domestic asylum process — transfers asylum seekers to France. Dimensional profile (AWR 3, EMP 3, ACT 3, EQU 2.5, BND 4, ACC 3, SYS 3, INT 3) consistent with micro-state functional placement: high BND (clearly defined small-state operational scope; strong rule-of-law) offset by EQU 2.5 (non-citizen-majority population access asymmetries and no domestic asylum process). Math reconstructs cleanly. Confirm at 51.6 / Functional with first-baseline rationale; Medium confidence.
key_evidence: Constitutional press freedom: government generally respects freedom of expression, including for press and media; independent judiciary and media landscape sustained.
Statutory exception: lèse-majesté laws criminalise insults/offences against the Prince or family with penalties up to 5 years' imprisonment — speech-restriction sub-threshold concern.

GRECO Second Compliance Group oversight: March 2026 (in-window for May 8 cycle) GRECO cited improvements in legislative-process transparency but flagged persistent gaps on parliamentarian financial-interest disclosure and judicial-appointment transparency (lack of conflict-of-interest rules).

July 2024 reforms: Monaco adopted asset-and-interest-disclosure regime and gift-reporting rule for executive members; established independent ethics committee with ethics advisor for confidential advice — positive ACC trajectory.

UN CERD 118th-session follow-up review: CERD accepting submissions for Monaco follow-up review (alongside Belarus, Bosnia, Ecuador, Kenya, Saudi Arabia, UK) — Monaco under active international human-rights scrutiny.
Population structure: foreign nationals constitute majority of Monaco's resident population; Monégasque citizens are minority; non-citizen-majority structural equity context.
Worker rights: foreign workers have rights to form/join independent unions, bargain collectively, conduct legal strikes; government workers prohibited from striking.
No domestic asylum process: asylum seekers transferred to France — boundary-of-state-protection scope-limit signal.
May 2026 Center for Global Law and Justice human-rights hearings calendar shows ongoing UN-treaty-body Monaco engagement (in-window).
No structural events in May 8 window that disrupt the baseline.

evidence.source: US State Department — 2024 Country Reports on Human Rights Practices: Monaco
evidence.finding: Constitutional press freedom respected; lèse-majesté statutory exception; GRECO Second Compliance Group oversight; July 2024 asset-disclosure reforms; CERD scrutiny.

evidence.source: Center for Global Law and Justice — Human Rights Bodies' Hearings & Sessions May 2026
evidence.finding: In-window indicator that UN treaty bodies (CERD 118th session) are actively engaging Monaco follow-up review.

evidence.source: Migrant Women Press — Migration without Rights is Exploitation (April 2026)
evidence.finding: Regional context on migrant-worker rights across European states; sub-threshold relevance to Monaco's foreign-national majority structure.

evidence.source: Business and Human Rights Centre — Migrant workers 2026 analysis
evidence.finding: In-window 2026 analysis of migrant-worker rights; structural baseline for European states.

evidence.source: US State Department — 2023 Country Reports on Human Rights Practices: Monaco
evidence.finding: Sustained constitutional rights and oversight framework documentation.

scoring_rationale.AWR: 3.0 sustained. Constitutional freedoms recognised; CERD treaty-body engagement provides external suffering-detection scaffolding. Anchor 3.
scoring_rationale.EMP: 3.0 sustained. State communications around minority and migrant issues generally measured; no documented affective-acknowledgment deficiencies. Anchor 3.
scoring_rationale.ACT: 3.0 sustained. Small-state action capacity proportionate to scale; healthcare, education, social-protection structures operational. Anchor 3.
scoring_rationale.EQU: 2.5 sustained. Non-citizen-majority population structure produces structural equity context; no domestic asylum process; migrant-domestic-worker concerns documented in regional 2026 analyses. Anchor 2-3.
scoring_rationale.BND: 4.0 sustained. Small-state boundary-of-protection clearly defined; rule-of-law strong; constitutional rights generally respected for citizens and long-term residents; structural micro-state boundary clarity. Anchor 4.
scoring_rationale.ACC: 3.0 sustained. July 2024 asset-disclosure reforms + independent ethics committee establish improving accountability trajectory; GRECO citations indicate persistent gaps on parliamentarian disclosure and judicial-appointment transparency. Net anchor 3.
scoring_rationale.SYS: 3.0 sustained. GRECO Council-of-Europe systemic scaffolding active; UN CERD treaty-body engagement; small-state systemic-governance proportionate. Anchor 3.
scoring_rationale.INT: 3.0 sustained. Declared rule-of-law and constitutional commitments consistent with operational practice; lèse-majesté statutory exception is sub-threshold INT concern (declared press freedom contradicted by 5-year-imprisonment provision for prince-criticism); ethics-committee establishment is positive INT signal. Anchor 3.

math_check.validator_check: Reconstructs cleanly to published 51.6. No math-hygiene flag. Sub-threshold delta. First agent baseline confirms functional-band placement.

negativeSignals: Lèse-majesté statutory speech-restriction (up to 5 years' imprisonment); GRECO-flagged persistent gaps on parliamentarian financial-interest disclosure and judicial-appointment conflict-of-interest rules; non-citizen-majority population access asymmetries; no domestic asylum process; migrant-domestic-worker regional concerns; government workers prohibited from striking.
positiveSignals: Constitutional press freedom operationally respected; independent judiciary and media landscape sustained; July 2024 asset-and-interest-disclosure reforms and independent ethics committee for executive; foreign workers have full union and collective-bargaining rights; small-state rule-of-law strong; UN CERD treaty-body engagement active; GRECO Council-of-Europe oversight scaffolding sustained.
sector_context: Countries — Western Europe / micro-state cluster. Cross-reference Liechtenstein, San Marino, Andorra, Vatican City. Monaco distinguished by high BND scope clarity (small-state rule-of-law) combined with EQU constraint (non-citizen-majority structure and no domestic asylum). Rotation backfill baseline confirms functional placement; no in-window structural events.
open_actions_resolved: First agent baseline established for Monaco at 51.6 / Functional
open_actions_carried: Watch: CERD 118th-session Monaco follow-up review outcomes
Watch: GRECO Third Compliance Round procedural progress on parliamentarian disclosure and judicial-appointment reforms
Watch: any lèse-majesté prosecutions (sub-threshold INT signal)
Watch: any migrant-domestic-worker enforcement actions or policy reforms
